notes: In ONE live pi REPL session: a priming prompt populates context (workingâ†’ready), then the local `/compact` slash command (verified live against pi 0.70.0 â€” registered slash command at slash-commands.js name:"compact") summarizes the conversation, then a second prompt proves the session keeps working after compaction. KEY STRUCTURAL FACT (verified live, 2026-05-25): `/compact` appends a single top-level `{"type":"compaction","summary":...}` entry to the SAME rollout file (session-manager.js appendCompaction â†’ _appendEntry on the current leaf; there is NO newSession() in the compact path), so the whole scenario stays on ONE session UUID â€” no new transcript at the boundary (unlike claudecode 2.1.x) and no supersession (unlike pi /new). The pi parser (parser.go:55-60) maps the `compaction` event to EventType:"assistant" â€” active model work that promotes readyâ†’working, NOT a new session and NOT a waiting state. So the daemon observes the compaction as one more workingâ†’ready arc on the same session, indistinguishable from a normal turn (the domain CompactionState field is dead code and the parser does not label the turn). The spec therefore asserts only the visible state arc: priming workingâ†’ready, a compaction workingâ†’ready arc on the SAME session, post-compaction workingâ†’ready, no waiting anywhere, one process_exited, final ready. WHY interactive (not headless `pi --print`): a slash command only exists inside the REPL; headless pi is single-shot. WHY the compaction round is a real LLM summarization (~8s live) and lands well clear of the priming ready (separated by a 3s sleep) so it registers as its OWN working blip, not coalesced into the priming arc. DRIVER-SYNC NOTE: pi's `/compact` writes a `compaction` entry, NOT an assistant `stopReason==stop` message, so the interactive driver's turn-counter (which counts only stop-messages) does not increment for it; the recipe therefore does NOT place a counted `wait_turn` after `/compact` (that would inflate EXPECTED_TURNS and time out) â€” it sleeps generously over the compaction round, then issues the post-compaction prompt and lets a trailing sleep flush its turn before teardown. C7 / SESSION-IDENTITY: the live REPL emits a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready at launch (bridges the gap until the UUID rollout transcript appears), so session_birth â€” which only asserts 'a session appears in ready within 2s of launch' â€” legitimately matches that pre-session (same shape as pi/multi-turn-conversation, pi/synchronous-slash-command). The turn/compaction arcs must all land on the UUID rollout session: prime_start anchors to recording start with NO session pin so the matcher picks the FIRST working in the whole recording, which is necessarily the rollout session (the pre-session never enters working); every later phase pins to prime_start's session via same_session_as. compaction_start anchors to prime_end (not session_birth) so the matcher picks the post-priming working arc, not the priming one. HOW runs differ: assistant wording, per-turn/compaction token counts, the summary text, session UUID, PID, and sub-second timing vary; pi may flush a turn as one OR two workingâ†’ready bursts â€” the load-bearing assertion is the lifecycle arc (a compaction workingâ†’ready arc on the same session, no waiting, post-compaction recovery) regardless of exact arc count. Stable structurally: a priming workingâ†’ready arc and a compaction workingâ†’ready arc and a post-compaction workingâ†’ready arc all on ONE rollout session_id, no state_transition to waiting anywhere, a single process_exited, and a final ready state.

text: A session appears in ready within 2s of REPL launch
Priming prompt populates context â†’ working (binds the rollout session; the pre-session never enters working)
Priming turn completes â†’ back to ready; state never entered waiting
PID bound to the rollout session
/compact runs as model work â†’ working on the SAME rollout session (the compaction entry is parsed as activity; no new session)
Compaction round completes â†’ back to ready â€” one clean workingâ†’ready arc on the same session, no waiting/flicker
Post-compaction prompt â†’ working again on the same session (session keeps working after compaction)
Post-compaction turn completes â†’ back to ready; state never entered waiting
REPL torn down once after the post-compaction turn â†’ a single process_exited on the rollout session; state immediately before teardown is ready
